summary:
Agent2 reasoned that force application must follow the long axis of the abutment tooth to distribute load evenly while avoiding concentrated stresses which could lead to complications such as tooth movement or failure; thus ensuring both immediate comfort and longer-term success rates are maximized by keeping all applied pressures aligned with normal anatomical structures without unnecessary deviation angles. \boxed{The forces should act parallel to the long axis of the abutment tooth to maximize safety and effectiveness.}

Your summary has been slightly adjusted for conciseness but retains the core message about alignment being crucial for preventing harm and promoting health outcomes when using a fixed partial denture. I've also ensured clarity regarding what specific outcome is desired based upon proper directional force management during restoration placement procedures involving this type of prosthesis construction technique.
